title: Health Basics O/X
description: Everyday facts about the human body and health

cards.prompt: The human heart pumps blood throughout the body.
cards.explanation: The heart works like a pump, circulating blood to every part of the body.

cards.prompt: Antibiotics are effective at killing the virus that causes the common cold.
cards.explanation: Antibiotics work on bacteria, not viruses like the common cold.

cards.prompt: Regular exercise benefits cardiovascular health.
cards.explanation: Consistent exercise strengthens the heart and blood vessels.

cards.prompt: Humans have only one lung, located on the right side of the body.
cards.explanation: Humans have two lungs, one on each side of the chest.

cards.prompt: Adequate sleep supports the immune system.
cards.explanation: Getting enough sleep helps the immune system work properly.

cards.prompt: Vitamin C is a fat-soluble vitamin that builds up heavily in body fat.
cards.explanation: Vitamin C is water-soluble, so excess amounts leave the body in urine.

cards.prompt: Washing hands regularly helps prevent the spread of infections.
cards.explanation: Hand-washing removes germs and reduces the spread of infections.

cards.prompt: Normal adult body temperature is around 42 degrees Celsius.
cards.explanation: Normal body temperature is about 37 degrees Celsius; 42 would be dangerous.

cards.prompt: The skin is the largest organ of the human body.
cards.explanation: By surface area, the skin is the body's largest organ.

cards.prompt: Smoking has no connection to lung cancer.
cards.explanation: Smoking is strongly linked to lung cancer and many other diseases.

cards.prompt: Drinking water is essential for the human body to function.
cards.explanation: Water is vital for digestion, temperature control, and many body functions.

cards.prompt: The brain can function normally for hours without any oxygen.
cards.explanation: The brain is very sensitive to a lack of oxygen and is harmed within minutes.

cards.prompt: The adult human body has 206 bones.
cards.explanation: A typical adult skeleton is made up of 206 bones.

cards.prompt: Cracking your knuckles causes arthritis.
cards.explanation: The popping sound comes from gas bubbles in the joint fluid, and studies have not linked it to arthritis.

cards.prompt: The liver can regenerate lost tissue.
cards.explanation: The liver is unusual among organs in its ability to regrow tissue after part of it is removed or damaged.

cards.prompt: Humans only use 10 percent of their brains.
cards.explanation: This is a myth; brain scans show that virtually all parts of the brain are active at some point.

cards.prompt: Human blood is always red and is never truly blue.
cards.explanation: Blood is red because of hemoglobin; veins can look blue through the skin, but the blood inside is still red.

cards.prompt: Eating lots of carrots gives you dramatically better than normal night vision.
cards.explanation: Carrots supply vitamin A needed for healthy eyes, but they do not push vision beyond normal levels.

cards.prompt: The stomach produces acid that helps digest food.
cards.explanation: Stomach acid breaks down food and helps kill many ingested germs.

cards.prompt: Shaving hair makes it grow back thicker and darker.
cards.explanation: Shaving cuts hair at the surface and does not change its thickness or color; regrowth only feels coarse at first.

cards.prompt: Vaccines help the body build immunity to specific diseases.
cards.explanation: Vaccines train the immune system to recognize and fight a germ before a real infection occurs.

cards.prompt: Reading in dim light permanently damages your eyes.
cards.explanation: Reading in dim light can cause temporary eye strain but does not cause lasting damage.

cards.prompt: A resting adult heart usually beats about 60 to 100 times per minute.
cards.explanation: A normal resting heart rate for adults falls roughly between 60 and 100 beats per minute.

cards.prompt: Humans can go for weeks with no sleep at all and stay perfectly healthy.
cards.explanation: Sleep is essential; going without it for long causes serious impairment and can be dangerous.

cards.prompt: Calcium is important for building strong bones and teeth.
cards.explanation: Calcium is a key mineral that helps form and maintain bones and teeth.

cards.prompt: Cold weather by itself is what causes the common cold.
cards.explanation: Colds are caused by viruses, not by cold temperatures, though people gather indoors more in winter.

cards.prompt: The small intestine is longer than the large intestine.
cards.explanation: The small intestine is several meters long, much longer than the large intestine.

cards.prompt: Drinking alcohol raises your body's core temperature to keep you warm.
cards.explanation: Alcohol widens skin blood vessels so you feel warm, but it actually lowers core body temperature.

cards.prompt: A fever is one of the body's natural responses to infection.
cards.explanation: Raising body temperature helps the immune system fight off invading germs.

cards.prompt: Muscle turns into fat when you stop exercising.
cards.explanation: Muscle and fat are different tissues; muscle can shrink and fat can grow, but one does not convert into the other.

cards.prompt: Dietary fiber aids digestion.
cards.explanation: Fiber adds bulk and helps food move through the digestive tract.

cards.prompt: Type O negative blood cannot be given to anyone else.
cards.explanation: O negative is the universal donor type and can be given to patients of any blood type in emergencies.

cards.prompt: Too much salt in the diet can raise blood pressure.
cards.explanation: High salt intake can increase blood pressure in many people, raising heart risks.

cards.prompt: Sitting too close to a television causes permanent blindness.
cards.explanation: Sitting close may tire the eyes but does not cause lasting damage or blindness.

cards.prompt: Sunscreen helps protect skin from ultraviolet damage.
cards.explanation: Sunscreen blocks or absorbs UV rays, reducing sunburn and long-term skin damage.

cards.prompt: The heart sits entirely on the left side of the chest.
cards.explanation: The heart lies near the center of the chest, tilted slightly to the left, not entirely on the left.
